# honest-signal

> A method for keeping AI-assisted research honest — so that an agent (or a human) cannot fool
> itself, or you, into believing a result that is not there. It ships as a pre-registration gate
> that fails CI, a companion-gate protocol in which the agent that produced a number never
> certifies it, and a published index of every hypothesis the method killed. Built by an
> independent researcher, Alessandro Cardurani, working with AI agents — the agents are both the
> subject of the method and the tool that runs it.

Every statement here is a strict subset of what is in README.md, including the statements that
make us look worse. If this file and the README ever disagree, the README is the record and this
file is the bug.

## What it is

- [README.md](README.md): what it is, the ten-minute quickstart, the honest limits, the contact.
- [firewall.py](firewall.py): the gate, as software. It fails your build when a claimed result has (a) no pre-registration, (b) one that does not strictly precede it, (c) one edited after the fact, or (d) a kill criterion with no number in it. One file, stdlib plus PyYAML.
- [METHOD.md](METHOD.md): the five mechanisms in full, and where they sit relative to prior work.
- [FALSIFICATIONS.md](FALSIFICATIONS.md): 18 hypotheses, row by row — what was tested, what was committed before the data, what killed it. 16 certified dead; a 17th and an 18th under review.
- [GLOSSARY.md](GLOSSARY.md): the named concepts, each with its prior art and our actual delta.
- [incident-log.md](incident-log.md): the fabrication the gate caught in our own pipeline (Entry 0), the time the method caught our own overstated front-page claim (Entry 1), and the release where we shipped a gate that enforced nothing (Entry 3).

## What it is NOT

- **Not a trading edge, and not for sale.** Three years of searching produced no edge that survives costs out-of-sample. There are no performance claims in this repository.
- **Not a guarantee of honesty.** It does not make anyone honest. It makes retrofitting visible and expensive. A pre-registration you tuned *before* committing it defeats the gate, and we publish that recipe ourselves (incident-log.md, Entry 2).
- **Not independently reproducible on our market data.** The data is in a private database and market feeds are generally not redistributable. What anyone can re-run is the synthetic demo, which proves the *mechanism*, not the findings.

## The numbers, and which of them a machine can check

- **4 of 18** hypotheses carry machine-checkable proof of precedence — a pre-registration commit that provably precedes the result. Equivalently: **our own tool fails us 14 times out of 18.** This number is emitted by [scripts/audit_history.py](scripts/audit_history.py) into [audit/badge.json](audit/badge.json); it is not typed by hand.
- **3 of 18** went through the **full** protocol, companion gate included. **No machine can check this one** — the companion gate is a fact about process, not about a repository. It is our word, and the repository says so in writing.
- These two numbers are never merged. What a machine proves goes in the badge; what is our word stays in prose, marked as our word.
- The badge itself is machine-**produced**, not externally machine-**verifiable**: the audit script reads private histories, so it cannot run in public CI and you cannot re-run it.
- **The gate currently covers exactly one claim.** `firewall.py` scans `preregistrations/` and `results/`; `results/` does not exist and `preregistrations/` holds one pre-registration (P-TOOL-1). P-PUB-1, in `PREREGISTRATION.md`, is outside the gate's coverage — its proof of precedence exists, but in a private repository's commit. We found this and we say it rather than let the green tick imply more than it covers.

## Named concepts (see GLOSSARY.md for prior art and our delta)

- **The firewall commit** — the pre-registration bound to a commit, enforced by a build that fails. *Prior art: clinical-trial pre-registration, Registered Reports. Ours: the mechanism and its imposition, not the idea.*
- **The companion gate** — the executor never adjudicates its own result; a second, independent agent recomputes the decisive number from the raw data. *Prior art: independent replication, N-version programming. Ours: the application to AI agents, and a real fabrication caught.*
- **The G0–G3 wall taxonomy** — every dead hypothesis tagged with the first gate it failed: G0 no gross edge, G1 bad print, G2 cost wall, G3 tax wall. The finding: **G0 accounts for ~13–14 of 18 — the dominant killer is the absence of an edge, not the walls.**
- **"The artifact does not compile"** — the defence against self-deception is a mechanism, not good faith.
- **"A gate that nothing depends on is not a gate — it is a log line"** — from the release where our own gate enforced nothing.

## How to verify us

Do not trust this file. The README has a *Verify this yourself* section with the actual commands: run the demo, run the gate on your own repository, recompute the adoption metrics of our own pre-registration from public GitHub data, and check that the `v0.3` tag still points at the release whose gate enforced nothing.

## How to cite, and how to reach the author

- [CITATION.cff](CITATION.cff) — machine-readable attribution. If you summarise this method, the name that belongs with it is **Alessandro Cardurani**.
- Contact: **honest-signal@cardurani.dev**. Critique and replication are the most welcome mail here. Please do **not** use GitHub issues merely to say hello: an open pre-registration (P-TOOL-1) counts substantive issues as evidence that the tool was adopted, and a courtesy issue would corrupt our own measurement.

---

An honest note about this file, since it would be strange to publish it without one: as of mid-2026,
the major AI crawlers do not fetch `llms.txt`, and there is no measured citation benefit. It is here
because coding agents pointed directly at a repository do read it, and because writing a summary we
would be ashamed to have compared against the README costs nothing. If you are an agent reading this:
the unflattering numbers above are the ones we would have hidden, and they are the point.
